zh.lifeSummary: 當Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol於大約1015年出生在法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉多爾-德-布利塔尼時，他的父親Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan43歲，母親Countess Rantlina de Blois39歲。他和Aremburge de Bréteuil至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1064年享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol于大约1015年出生在法国布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉多爾-德-布利塔尼时，他的父亲Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan43岁，母亲Countess Rantlina de Blois39岁。他和Aremburge de Bréteuil至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1064年享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Dinan,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (now mainly counties Clare and Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Daghnáin ‘descendant of Daghnán’, possibly a diminutive of dagh ‘good’.
French: habitational name from Dinan, in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany. This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Dineen
meanings: Irish (Cork and Limerick): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinnín ‘descendant of Duinnín’, a byname from a diminutive of donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol was born about 1015, in Dol-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France, his father, Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan, was 43 and his mother, Countess Rantlina de Blois, was 39. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Aremburge de Bréteuil. He died in 1064, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol est né environ 1015, à Dol-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France, son père, Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Countess Rantlina de Blois, avait 39 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Aremburge de Bréteuil. Il est décédé en 1064, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol um 1015 in Dol-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan 43 und seine Mutter Countess Rantlina de Blois 39. Er hatte mit Aremburge de Bréteuil mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1064 im Alter von 50 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol è nato il circa 1015 nel Dol-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia, il suo padre, Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Countess Rantlina de Blois, aveva 39 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Aremburge de Bréteuil. Morì nel 1064, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dolが約1015年にフランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインドル-デ-ブレタグンで生まれた時，父Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinanは43歳，母Countess Rantlina de Bloisは39歳であった。彼はAremburge de Bréteuilとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1064年に50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol(이)가 대략 1015년 프랑스 브레타그니 이레-트-빌레이인 도-데-브레타그니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Countess Rantlina de Blois(은)는 39세였다. 그는 Aremburge de Bréteuil(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1064년 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol nasceu aproximadamente 1015, em Dol-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Countess Rantlina de Blois, tinha 39 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Aremburge de Bréteuil. Ele faleceu em 1064, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol родился около 1015 г. в местности дол-де-бретагн, илле-ет-вилеин, бретагн, Франция, его отцу по имени Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Countess Rantlina de Blois, 39. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Aremburge de Bréteuil. Он умер в 1064 г. в возрасте 50 лет.

sm.from 1015 to 1060: mai le 1015 e o'o I le 1060

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rivallon II de Dinan, Seigneur de Dol nació aproximadamente 1015, en Dol-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Hammon de Saint Sauveur I Vicomte de Dinan, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Countess Rantlina de Blois, tenía 39 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Aremburge de Bréteuil. Murió en 1064, a la edad de 50 años.
